---
name: Directive
about: A Product Owner directive — one unit of work for the AI development team
title: "[Directive] "
labels: directive
---

## Directive

<!-- The intent in plain language: what should change, and why. Normally
     captured here by the main session from the conversation with the
     Product Owner; the PO may also fill it directly. The "how" is not
     needed — the specification handles that. -->

## Specification

<!-- For a full task: written by the spec-analyst. For a lightweight task:
     a one-line spec written by the main session.
     - Goal
     - Scope (included / not included)
     - Acceptance criteria (a checklist of testable statements)
     - Open questions -->

## Context & references

<!-- Filled before building, so the builder has everything and need not
     re-research:
     - Research findings — links into docs/developer/research/
     - Feature entry — the feature ID and link, if this is feature work
     - Related ADRs and contracts -->

## Gate 1 — Spec sign-off (Product Owner)

- [ ] I confirm the specification reflects my intent. Build may start.

## Gate 2 — Delivery acceptance

<!-- Gate 2 = PR-Approval on the linked pull request. Branch protection on
     main enforces it before merge. See CLAUDE.md, The Two Gates. -->

## Process log

<!-- Filled by the main session at Close. Bullet list, Metric: value:
     - Review rounds:
     - Gate 1 rejections:
     - Gate 2 rejections:
     - Escalations:
     - Notes: -->

- Review rounds:
- Gate 1 rejections:
- Gate 2 rejections:
- Escalations:
- Notes:
